description:
REQ-031 Phase 3, item 11: Add event-driven trigger declarations so workflows can be dispatched automatically in response to git events, schedules, or custom events.

Changes needed:
1. Add `TriggerDefinition` type to workflow_config.rs:
   - `on_push: Option<PushTriggerConfig>` with branch filter (e.g., main only)
   - `on_pr: Option<PrTriggerConfig>` with event filter (opened, merged, closed)
   - `on_schedule: Option<ScheduleTriggerConfig>` with cron expression or interval
   - `on_custom: Option<CustomTriggerConfig>` with event name for manual dispatch
2. Add `triggers: Vec<TriggerDefinition>` to `PipelineDefinition` in workflow_config.rs
3. Add trigger evaluation in daemon_scheduler_project_tick.rs:
   - On each tick, check git state changes (new commits on main, new branches, PR events via gh CLI)
   - Check schedule triggers against last-run timestamps
   - If trigger fires, auto-create a task and start a workflow for the matching pipeline
4. Store trigger state in `.ao/state/trigger-state.json` (last evaluated commit SHA, last schedule run)
5. Add `ao workflow trigger list` and `ao workflow trigger fire <pipeline-id>` CLI commands
6. Add YAML support: `on: { push: { branches: [main] }, schedule: { cron: "0 9 * * *" } }`
7. Add tests: push trigger fires on new main commit, schedule trigger respects interval, trigger doesn't re-fire for same commit

Note: This overlaps with REQ-030 (Proactive Daemon Scheduling). The schedule trigger here focuses on workflow dispatch; REQ-030 covers broader proactive daemon behavior.

Key files:
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/workflow_config.rs (PipelineDefinition)
- crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_scheduler_project_tick.rs
- crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_git_helpers.rs (git state detection)
title: Add trigger declarations for event-driven workflow dispatch
